body:
## Summary

Incremental progress toward interrupt-driven xHCI input. Lands the infrastructure without removing the polling fallback yet — full polling removal requires deferring the SPI activation until after all subsystems init, which will be a follow-up (Phase 5+).

## What's in here

### Phase 1: Linux-probe MSI ground truth (docs only)
- \`docs/planning/f32t-xhci-msi/linux-ground-truth.md\` — evidence from linux-probe that xHCI MSI → GICv2m SPI → xhci_msi_irq works on the same Parallels ARM64 hypervisor, 14-54µs latency

### Phase 2: Linux-order PCI MSI programming (\`pci.rs\`)
Refactor \`Device::configure_msi()\` to match Linux's sequence:
1. Clear MSI Enable bit
2. Mask vector 0 if per-vector masking supported
3. Clear Qsize (single-vector mode)
4. Write Message Address Lo (and Hi=0 for 64-bit)
5. Write Message Data
6. Read back flags (posted-write flush)
7. Disable INTx (now internal to configure_msi)
8. Set MSI Enable
9. Unmask vector 0

Previously Breenix wrote msg/mask then Enable then INTx off — this ordering is the suspected cause of the historical "MSI storm" that led commit 488d2fc2 to defensively set \`XhciState.irq=0\`.

Linux parity:
- \`/tmp/linux-v6.8/drivers/pci/msi/msi.c:359-389\` \`__pci_write_msi_msg\`
- \`/tmp/linux-v6.8/drivers/pci/msi/msi.c:184-204\` \`pci_msi_update_mask\`

Call sites updated (AHCI, xHCI, virtio-gpu, virtio-net) to remove now-redundant external \`disable_intx()\` invocations.

### Phase 4: \`XhciState.irq = early_irq\` (\`usb/xhci.rs\`)
The MSI irq was always allocated but \`state.irq\` was hardcoded to 0, which kept the deferred SPI activation path gated off. Plumb it correctly. No runtime effect yet because SPI activation still only runs inside poll_hid_events.

## What's NOT in here (follow-up)

- Phase 5: delete \`poll_hid_events\` and the 200Hz timer-driven polling
- Phase 6: migrate BWM off \`graphics::mouse_pos()\` / \`poll_modifier_state()\` polling onto waitqueue wake

First attempt at Phase 5 (enabling SPI inline at xhci::init() completion) was reverted — it fires too early, before AHCI/FS/scheduler finish initialization, causing disk reads to stall. Needs a deferred trigger tied to system-ready, not a poll counter.

## Validation

- aarch64 clean build
- User confirmed: desktop boots, cursor works, frame rates normal, CPU utilization as expected (pre-existing 800% from idle gate — separate F32s work)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commits.messageBody:
Captured from linux-probe (10.211.55.3) on same Parallels ARM64 hypervisor:
- Linux xhci_hcd uses MSI → GICv2m SPI → xhci_msi_irq successfully
- MSI address 0x02250040, SPI 56, edge-triggered, Group 1
- Mesa per-frame runs through IRQ, not polling; no timer-driven fallback

Used as Phase 2 reference for Breenix's PCI MSI programming order fix.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: docs(kernel): F32t Phase 1 Linux xHCI MSI ground truth

commits.messageBody:
Refactor Device::configure_msi() to match Linux's PCI MSI programming
sequence (drivers/pci/msi/msi.c:359-389, :184-204):

1. Read Message Control flags (64-bit, mask capability)
2. Clear MSI Enable bit (disable during setup)
3. Mask vector 0 if per-vector masking supported
4. Clear Qsize (single-vector mode)
5. Write Message Address Lo (and Hi=0 for 64-bit)
6. Write Message Data
7. Read back flags (posted-write flush)
8. Disable INTx (PCI_COMMAND.INTX_DISABLE)
9. Enable MSI
10. Unmask vector 0

Previously Breenix wrote address/data/mask then Enable then INTx off, which
could leave stale pending MSI signals at the enable moment — the suspected
source of the historical MSI storm that led commit 488d2fc2 to set
XhciState.irq=0 defensively.

Named constants extracted (PCI_COMMAND_*, PCI_MSI_FLAGS_*, etc.) and
configure_msi() now calls disable_intx() internally. Updated call sites
(AHCI, xHCI, virtio-gpu, virtio-net) to remove the now-redundant external
disable_intx() invocation.

Linux parity:
- /tmp/linux-v6.8/drivers/pci/msi/msi.c:359-389 __pci_write_msi_msg
- /tmp/linux-v6.8/drivers/pci/msi/msi.c:184-204 pci_msi_update_mask

Validation: aarch64 kernel builds clean. No runtime behavior change expected
beyond the MSI programming order; subsequent phases (state.irq enablement,
polling removal, BWM wake migration) follow.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: fix(pci): F32t Phase 2 Linux-order MSI programming

commits.messageBody:
With F32t Phase 2's Linux-order MSI programming now in place (disable → mask →
write → flush → INTx off → enable → unmask), the historical "MSI storm"
condition that led commit 488d2fc2 to defensively set XhciState.irq=0 should
no longer occur. Storms came from enabling MSI while pending vectors were
still latched; Linux ordering clears and masks before enabling.

Set state.irq = early_irq so the deferred SPI activation path at
kernel/src/drivers/usb/xhci.rs:6183-6186 calls gic::enable_spi(state.irq) and
the xHCI interrupt is delivered via MSI → GIC SPI → handle_interrupt().

After this commit, the timer-driven poll_hid_events() path is no longer the
primary HID event driver — MSI delivery is. The polling code remains for now
and will be deleted in F32t Phase 5 once MSI delivery is validated.

Linux parity: /tmp/linux-v6.8/drivers/usb/host/xhci.c::xhci_try_enable_msi
enables MSI during init; /tmp/linux-v6.8/drivers/pci/msi/msi.c::__pci_msi_desc_mask_irq
is the unmask step in the enable sequence.

Validation: clean aarch64 kernel build. Full Parallels boot test pending.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: fix(usb): F32t Phase 4 enable xHCI MSI delivery

commits.messageBody:
Previously the GIC SPI for xHCI was enabled lazily inside poll_hid_events()
when its poll counter reached 50 (~250ms after init). That coupled MSI
activation to the timer-driven polling path.

Move SPI enable to the end of xhci::init(), after:
- PCI MSI capability programmed with Linux-order sequence (Phase 2)
- state.irq = early_irq stored (Phase 4)
- IMAN.IE = 1, USBCMD.RS=1 set
- Enumeration complete, TRBs queued for HID endpoints

This is where Linux enables interrupts at the end of controller bring-up
(drivers/usb/host/xhci.c::xhci_run_finished_at). poll_hid_events() no longer
holds the only path to activation; Phase 5b will delete that function.

Validation: clean aarch64 kernel build. Runtime test pending.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: fix(usb): F32t Phase 5a move xHCI SPI enable to end of init
Revert "fix(usb): F32t Phase 5a move xHCI SPI enable to end of init"

title: F32t Phase 1-4: Linux-order PCI MSI programming + xHCI state.irq plumbing
